property.name: 17477a69-d9a5-4251-9329-0963e8121b5f
property.value:
- Commands and Queries should NOT be treated like DTOs for Service Operation elements. They are independent of Service elements, with their own infrastructure. - Commands and Queries describe the payloads. They imply the payload, it's handler and validators. If they are exposed over https, then they represent a DTO for those requests and represent the infrastructure for the http endpoints. They are made up of DTO-Field properties. - If applying http endpoints to Commands and Queries, use the fully qualified route (e.g.`api/orders/{orderId}`) - Always put Commands and Queries into an appropriate folder (e.g. Orders, Buyers, etc.)
## Service Diagram Layout Rules: - Always lay out newly created Commands and Queries on the current diagram. - Place all Commands and Queries in a single vertical column on the left side of the diagram. - Space each Command and Query exactly 100px apart vertically, measured from the top of one node to the top of the next. - Place related Domain entities to the right of the Commands/Queries they support, grouped by feature area. - Always include and route interaction associations on the diagram so the linked Domain entity is visually aligned with its related operations.
